description: Detects reasoning patterns that equate magnitude—physical, numerical, or economic—with inherent strength or superiority. Flags when mass is confused with resilience or wisdom.

linguistic: size-as-strength claims ('bigger is stronger', 'small means weak')
hierarchical metaphors ('top of the food chain', 'giant among men')
dismissal of small-scale efficiency ('too small to matter')
empirical_structural: failure propagation rate in large vs. small systems
behavioral_cultural: investment bias toward concentration not distribution
use of intimidation or spectacle as proof of power

fusion_logic.method: multi-domain correlation between scale metrics and resilience outcomes

thresholds.high (>0.6): size-dominance frailty – equating mass with strength

recommended_reframe: Evaluate strength as stress-absorption and recovery speed, not mass.
Highlight micro-scale resilience models (cells, ecosystems, communities).
Substitute intimidation metrics with adaptability metrics.
